principle: Complex synonyms add no meaning — replace with the simplest word that carries the same idea
explanation: X.copy|complex_synonyms: same_concept often has simple + complex forms; complex != smarter; pick_shortest_familiar_word; NO:using_latin_root_when_native_exists eg: "personnel_resource_demonstrated_positive_productivity_tendency" >> "team_started_closing_deals_faster"

principle: Inflated adjectives evaluate instead of showing — replace dramatic/critical/tectonic with facts
explanation: X.copy|inflated_adjectives: "dramatic/critical/tectonic/phenomenal/catastrophic" -> empty_evaluation; reader_cant_feel_scale_from_adjective; show_with_facts_instead; NO:decorating_mundane_with_epic_words eg: "dramatic_sales_growth" >> "monthly_revenue_equaled_annual_target"

principle: Pop psychology terms obscure what actually happened — describe the situation plainly
explanation: X.copy|pop_psychology: "subconscious/gestalt/projection/abusive/narcissist" -> borrowed_terms_used_loosely; easy_to_misuse + reader_may_not_share_framework; describe_observable_behavior_instead; NO:diagnosing_via_buzzwords eg: "client_projects_abusive_narcissist_behavior" >> "client_gives_contradictory_feedback"

principle: Jargon hides bad news from accountability — simplify to expose the real situation
explanation: X.copy|jargon_shield: complex_language -> obscures_problems; reader_skims_without_grasping_severity; also: myth_that_bosses_need_formal_language -> ask_them; NO:wrapping_failure_in_latinisms eg: "resource_consolidation_demonstrated_unsatisfactory_motivation_effect" >> "we_raised_salaries_but_still_behind_plan_by_a_month"

principle: Same word can be jargon or precise term — keep when it's the real discipline, cut when it's decoration
explanation: X.copy|term_vs_jargon: word_in_its_discipline == precise_term -> keep; same_word_outside_discipline == decoration -> replace; NO:blanket_banning_complex_words; context_decides eg: "audit_of_consumer_patterns" (jargon) vs "audit_by_accounting_firm" (term); "dominant_theme" (jargon) vs "dominant_gene" (biology)
